records.ety: [OE. gobelin, F. gobelin, LL. gobelinus, fr. Gr. <?/ knave, a mischievous goblin; or cf. G. kobold, E. kobold, cobalt, Armor. gobilin an ignis fatuus, goblin.]

defs: An evil or mischievous spirit; a playful or malicious elf; a frightful phantom; a gnome.
quotes: To whom the goblin, full of wrath, replied.
